data.description: Modern standard astronomical epoch used for many star catalogs and celestial coordinate references.
data.usage_note: Recommended default for storing canonical fixed-star source coordinates.

data.description: Older Besselian epoch formerly used in many astronomical catalogs.
data.usage_note: Kept mainly for compatibility with older astronomical sources.

data.description: Future Julian epoch sometimes used for projected coordinates.
data.usage_note: Useful only if you store projected future positions.

data.description: Coordinates calculated for the exact runtime date instead of a fixed catalog epoch.
data.usage_note: Use for calculated chart/runtime positions, not for immutable source catalog data.
